reasoning: The document contains one major LLM fidelity defect where bulleted lists within table cells have been flattened into plain text rather than using proper HTML list elements (<ul>/<li>), which breaks accessibility for screen readers. There is also a minor programmatic observation regarding table header semantics. According to the count-based routing rules, 1 major defect requires HUMAN_REVIEW.

concerns.analysis: The source document contains several bulleted lists under the agenda topics (e.g., under 'Project Finance Update'). In the output JSON, these lists are flattened into plain text strings containing bullet characters (\u2022) separated by newlines within the table cells, rather than being structured as proper HTML list elements (<ul>/<li>). This breaks accessibility for screen readers.

Project Finance Update
• Cost share payments received.

concerns.output_quote: "text": "Project Finance Update\n• Cost share payments received.\n• Funds expended."

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 1 table(s) have time values in <th> cells: "10:00-10:10"
concerns.analysis: data_in_th_time=1, no_th_tables=0. Minor semantic markup issues: tables lack <th> elements or use time values as headers. These affect screen reader context but are less severe than data misrepresentation.

action_items: Convert flattened text bullet points inside table cells into properly formatted <ul>/<li> HTML list elements.
Verify and adjust table headers (<th>) to ensure they contain column descriptions rather than data like time values.
